function.description: Permanently removes a specific reaction from an issue in a GitHub repository.
function.name: GITHUB_DELETE_ISSUE_REACTION

parameters.description: Request schema for deleting a reaction from an issue in a GitHub repository.

issue_number.description: The unique number identifying the issue from which the reaction will be deleted.
issue_number.title: Issue Number

owner.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.
owner.title: Owner

reaction_id.description: The unique identifier of the reaction to be deleted.
reaction_id.title: Reaction Id

repo.description: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.
repo.title: Repo

parameters.title: DeleteIssueReactionRequest

function.description: Permanently removes an existing label from a repository.
function.name: GITHUB_DELETE_LABEL

parameters.description: Request schema for `DeleteLabel`

name.description: The name of the label to delete. Label names are case-insensitive. If the label name contains spaces or special characters, ensure it is correctly URL-encoded (e.g., 'help wanted' becomes 'help%20wanted').
name.title: Name

owner.description: REQUIRED. The username or organization name that owns the repository. GitHub repositories are identified by owner/repo (e.g., 'octocat/Hello-World'), so both 'owner' and 'repo' must be provided together. This field is not case-sensitive.
owner.title: Owner

repo.description: REQUIRED. The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. Must be provided together with 'owner' to identify the repository (e.g., for 'github.com/octocat/Hello-World', repo is 'Hello-World'). This field is not case-sensitive.
repo.title: Repo

parameters.title: DeleteLabelRequest

function.description: Deletes the specified milestone if it exists; this operation is idempotent, typically returning a 404 if the milestone is not found or already deleted.

milestone_number.description: The unique number that identifies the milestone to be deleted.

owner.title: Owner

repo.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.
repo.title: Repo

parameters.title: DeleteMilestoneRequest

function.description: Permanently deletes a specific codespace belonging to a member of the specified organization.

username.description: GitHub username of the user owning the codespace.
username.title: Username

parameters.title: DeleteOrgCodespaceRequest

function.description: Deletes a specific package in an organization; cannot delete public packages with over 5,000 downloads.
function.name: GITHUB_DELETE_ORG_PACKAGE

parameters.description: Parameters for deleting a specific package within an organization.

org.description: Name of the organization owning the package (not case-sensitive).
org.title: Org

package_name.description: Unique name of the package to be deleted.
package_name.title: Package Name

package_type.description: Type of the package to be deleted. Packages in GitHub's Gradle registry have type `maven`. Docker images in GitHub's Container registry (`ghcr.io`) have type `container`. Use type `docker` for images previously in GitHub's Docker registry (`docker.pkg.github.com`), even if migrated to the Container registry.
package_type.title: Package Type

parameters.title: DeleteOrgPackageRequest

function.description: Permanently deletes a specific package owned by the authenticated user; public packages downloaded over 5,000 times cannot be deleted via this API.
function.name: GITHUB_DELETE_PACKAGE

parameters.description: Request schema for deleting a package for the authenticated user.

package_name.description: The unique name of the package to delete. For npm: supports scoped packages like '@scope/name'. For maven: use format 'com.example.package'. For container/docker: use image name like 'my-app'. The name must match exactly as it appears in the GitHub Packages registry.
package_name.title: Package Name

package_type.description: The type of package to delete. Supported types: 'npm' (Node.js packages), 'maven' (Java/Gradle packages), 'rubygems' (Ruby gems), 'docker' (legacy Docker images from docker.pkg.github.com), 'nuget' (.NET packages), 'container' (Docker images from ghcr.io). Note: GitHub Gradle packages use 'maven' type; ghcr.io images use 'container' type.
package_type.title: Package Type

parameters.title: DeletePackageRequest

function.description: Tool to delete a package version using GitHub GraphQL API. Use when you need to remove a specific version of a package by its global node ID.
function.name: GITHUB_DELETE_PACKAGE_VERSION

parameters.description: Request schema for deleting a package version via GraphQL

client_mutation_id.description: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation. Used to ensure idempotency of mutations.
client_mutation_id.title: Client Mutation Id

package_version_id.description: The global node ID of the package version to delete (e.g., 'MDEyOlBhY2thZ2VWZXJzaW9uMQ=='). This is the GraphQL ID, not the numeric REST API ID.
package_version_id.title: Package Version Id

parameters.title: DeletePackageVersionRequest

function.description: Deletes a specific package version within an organization; requires admin permissions for packages with over 5,000 downloads.
function.name: GITHUB_DELETE_PACKAGE_VERSION_FOR_AN_ORGANIZATION

parameters.description: Request schema for `DeletePackageVersionForAnOrganization`

package_type.description:
Package ecosystem. Notes:
- `maven`: For GitHub's Gradle registry packages.
- `container`: For Docker images in GitHub's Container registry (ghcr.io).
- `docker`: For images originally in GitHub's Docker registry (docker.pkg.github.com), including migrated ones.
package_type.title: Package Type

package_version_id.description: Unique ID of the package version to delete.
package_version_id.title: Package Version Id

parameters.title: DeletePackageVersionForAnOrganizationRequest

function.description: Permanently and irreversibly deletes a specific version of a package owned by the specified user.
function.name: GITHUB_DELETE_PACKAGE_VERSION_FOR_A_USER

parameters.description: Request schema for deleting a specific version of a package owned by a user.

package_name.description: The name of the package to which the version belongs.
package_name.title: Package Name

package_type.description: The type of the package. Supported types include npm, maven, rubygems, docker, nuget, and container. Packages in GitHub's Gradle registry use the `maven` type. Docker images in GitHub's Container registry (ghcr.io) use the `container` type. The `docker` type can be used for images previously in GitHub's Docker registry (docker.pkg.github.com), even if migrated.
package_type.title: Package Type

package_version_id.description: The unique identifier of the package version to be deleted.
package_version_id.title: Package Version Id

username.description: The GitHub username of the account that owns the package.
username.title: Username

parameters.title: DeletePackageVersionForAUserRequest

function.description: Deletes a pending (unsubmitted) review from a pull request; this is only possible if the review has not yet been submitted.
function.name: GITHUB_DELETE_PENDING_REVIEW

parameters.description: Request schema for `DeletePendingReview`

owner.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.
owner.title: Owner

pull_number.description: The number that identifies the pull request.
pull_number.title: Pull Number

repo.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.
repo.title: Repo

review_id.description: The unique identifier of the pending review to be deleted.
review_id.title: Review Id

parameters.title: DeletePendingReviewRequest

function.description: Deletes the specified GitHub project (Projects V2) using the GraphQL API. Note: This action uses GitHub's Projects V2 GraphQL API. The legacy Projects (classic) REST API was sunset on April 1, 2025 and is no longer available. The project is permanently deleted and cannot be recovered. The user must have admin permissions for the project to delete it.
function.name: GITHUB_DELETE_PROJECT

parameters.description: Request schema for deleting a GitHub project.

project_id.description: The unique GraphQL node ID of the project to be deleted (e.g., 'PVT_kwDOBcMqx84ABdPx'). This is returned from project creation or listing operations.
project_id.title: Project Id

parameters.title: DeleteProjectRequest

function.description: Deletes a project card from a GitHub 'Project (classic)'; this operation is idempotent. DEPRECATION NOTICE: GitHub Classic Projects (V1) and its REST API were sunset on April 1, 2025. This action may return a 404 error on GitHub.com. For new projects, use GitHub Projects V2 which uses the GraphQL API instead. See: https://github.blog/changelog/2024-05-23-sunset-notice-projects-classic/
function.name: GITHUB_DELETE_PROJECT_CARD

parameters.description: Request schema for deleting a project card.

card_id.description: The unique numerical identifier of the project card to be deleted.
card_id.title: Card Id

parameters.title: DeleteProjectCardRequest

function.description: Deletes a project field (column) from a GitHub Projects V2 project using the GraphQL API. IMPORTANT: GitHub Classic Projects REST API was sunset on April 1, 2025. This action now uses the GitHub Projects V2 GraphQL API with the deleteProjectV2Field mutation. Note: The built-in Status field cannot be deleted as it is a core field in GitHub Projects V2. Only custom fields can be deleted.
function.name: GITHUB_DELETE_PROJECT_COLUMN

parameters.description:
Request schema for deleting a specific project field (column) by its ID.

Note: This action uses GitHub Projects V2 GraphQL API. The Classic Projects REST API
was sunset on April 1, 2025.

field_id.description: The node ID of the project field to be deleted. This is a GraphQL node ID (e.g., 'PVTF_...').
field_id.title: Field Id

parameters.title: DeleteProjectColumnRequest

function.description: Tool to delete a project item for a user in GitHub Projects V2. Use when you need to remove an item from a user's project. Requires the project item's GraphQL node ID (starts with 'PVTI_'). Uses the GitHub GraphQL API deleteProjectV2Item mutation.
function.name: GITHUB_DELETE_PROJECT_ITEM_FOR_USER

parameters.description: Request model for deleting a project item for a user.

item_id.description: The GraphQL node ID of the project item to delete (e.g. 'PVTI_lADOBCxhzs4A...').
item_id.title: Item Id

username.description: The handle for the GitHub user account.
username.title: Username

parameters.title: DeleteProjectItemForUserRequest

function.description: Removes a repository from the list of selected repositories enabled for GitHub Actions in an organization. Only works when the organization's `enabled_repositories` policy is set to `selected`. Requires admin:org scope.

parameters.description: Request schema for `DisableRepositoryActionsInOrg`

repository_id.description: The unique identifier of the repository.
repository_id.title: Repository Id

parameters.title: DisableRepositoryActionsInOrgRequest

function.description: Dismisses an APPROVED or CHANGES_REQUESTED review on a pull request with a mandatory explanatory message. IMPORTANT: Only reviews in APPROVED or CHANGES_REQUESTED state can be dismissed. Reviews in COMMENTED, PENDING, or already DISMISSED state will return a 422 error. To dismiss a review on a protected branch, you must be a repository admin or be authorized to dismiss pull request reviews.
function.name: GITHUB_DISMISS_A_REVIEW_FOR_A_PULL_REQUEST

parameters.description:
Request schema for `DismissAReviewForAPullRequest`

Note: Only reviews in APPROVED or CHANGES_REQUESTED state can be dismissed.
Reviews in COMMENTED, PENDING, or DISMISSED state cannot be dismissed.

event.description: The event type for dismissing the review. Must be set to DISMISS.
event.title: EventEnm

message.description: The reason for dismissing the review. This is a required field.
examples: All concerns addressed by recent commits.
Review no longer applicable after refactoring
message.title: Message

owner.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.
owner.title: Owner

pull_number.description: The unique number identifying the pull request within the repository.
pull_number.title: Pull Number

repo.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.
repo.title: Repo

review_id.description: The unique identifier for the review to be dismissed. The review must be in APPROVED or CHANGES_REQUESTED state.
review_id.title: Review Id

parameters.title: DismissAReviewForAPullRequestRequest

function.description: Downloads a GitHub Actions workflow artifact as a ZIP file. Returns the artifact as a downloadable file. Use GITHUB_LIST_ARTIFACTS_FOR_A_REPOSITORY or GITHUB_LIST_WORKFLOW_RUN_ARTIFACTS to get valid artifact IDs first.
function.name: GITHUB_DOWNLOAD_AN_ARTIFACT

parameters.description: Request schema for `DownloadAnArtifact`

archive_format.description: Archive format for the download. GitHub API only supports 'zip'. Defaults to 'zip'.
archive_format.title: Archive Format

artifact_id.description: Unique numeric identifier of the artifact. Obtain from GITHUB_LIST_ARTIFACTS_FOR_A_REPOSITORY or GITHUB_LIST_WORKFLOW_RUN_ARTIFACTS actions.
artifact_id.title: Artifact Id

owner.description: Username or organization name of the repository owner (case-insensitive).
owner.title: Owner

repo.description: Repository name, without the `.git` extension (case-insensitive).
repo.title: Repo

parameters.title: DownloadAnArtifactRequest

function.description: Downloads a repository's source code as a tarball (.tar.gz) archive for a specific Git reference, if the repository is accessible.
function.name: GITHUB_DOWNLOAD_A_REPOSITORY_ARCHIVE_TAR

parameters.description: Request schema for `DownloadARepositoryArchiveTar`

owner.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.
owner.title: Owner

ref.description: A Git reference (e.g., branch name, tag name, or commit SHA) that points to the specific version of the repository content to be downloaded as a tarball archive. For example, `main` for the main branch, `v1.0.0` for a tag, or a full commit SHA.
ref.title: Ref

repo.description: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. The name is not case sensitive.
repo.title: Repo

parameters.title: DownloadARepositoryArchiveTarRequest

function.description: Downloads a repository's source code as a ZIP archive for a specific Git reference (branch, tag, or commit SHA). IMPORTANT SIZE LIMITATION: This action may fail with 'payload too large' errors for large repositories due to platform size restrictions. If you encounter size limit issues, consider these alternatives: - Use GITHUB_GET_REPOSITORY_CONTENT to fetch specific files or directories (also lists directory contents) - Clone the repository using git if you need the full codebase Best suited for: small to medium repositories, downloading specific tagged releases, or quick code inspection.
function.name: GITHUB_DOWNLOAD_A_REPOSITORY_ARCHIVE_ZIP

parameters.description: Request schema for downloading a repository's archive in ZIP format.

owner.description: The username of the account that owns the repository. This is not case-sensitive.
owner.title: Owner

ref.description: The Git reference for the archive. This can be a branch name, a tag name, or a commit SHA.
ref.title: Ref

repo.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This is not case-sensitive.
repo.title: Repo

parameters.title: DownloadARepositoryArchiveZipRequest

function.description: Enables private vulnerability reporting for a repository, allowing security researchers to privately submit vulnerability reports to maintainers.

parameters.description: Request schema for enabling private vulnerability reporting for a repository.

owner.description: The account owner of the repository. This is typically the username or organization name and is not case-sensitive.
owner.title: Owner

repo.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.
repo.title: Repo

parameters.title: GithubEnablePrivateVulnReportingForRepoRequest

function.description: Adds a repository to the list of selected repositories enabled for GitHub Actions in an organization. Requires the organization's 'enabled_repositories' policy to be set to 'selected'.
function.name: GITHUB_ENABLE_REPO_FOR_GITHUB_ACTIONS

parameters.description: Specifies the target organization and repository for enabling GitHub Actions.

org.description: The name of the GitHub organization. This name is not case-sensitive.
org.title: Org

repository_id.description: The unique numerical identifier of the GitHub repository.
repository_id.title: Repository Id

parameters.title: EnableRepoForGithubActionsRequest

function.description: Creates or updates a repository's development environment secret using an `encrypted_value` and its corresponding `key_id`; the secret must be pre-encrypted with the repository's Codespaces public key.
function.name: GITHUB_ENCRYPT_AND_UPDATE_DEV_SECRET

parameters.description: Request schema for creating or updating a development environment secret with an encrypted value.

encrypted_value.description: Value for your secret, already encrypted using the public key retrieved from the GitHub API's 'Get a repository public key for Codespaces' endpoint (`GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/codespaces/secrets/public-key`). Use this if you have pre-encrypted the value yourself.
encrypted_value.title: Encrypted Value

key_id.description: ID of the public key (retrieved from the GitHub API's 'Get a repository public key for Codespaces' endpoint: `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/codespaces/secrets/public-key`) used to encrypt the secret's value. If not provided, it will be fetched automatically.
key_id.title: Key Id

owner.description: The account owner of the repository. This name is not case-sensitive.
owner.title: Owner

repo.description: The name of the repository without the `.git` extension. This name is not case-sensitive.
repo.title: Repo

secret_name.description: The name of the development environment secret.
secret_name.title: Secret Name

secret_value.description: The plaintext value for your secret. This will be automatically encrypted using the repository's Codespaces public key before being sent to GitHub. If you already have an encrypted value, use the encrypted_value parameter instead.
secret_value.title: Secret Value

parameters.title: EncryptAndUpdateDevSecretRequest

function.description: Creates or updates an organization's GitHub Codespaces secret using an encrypted value and its corresponding public key ID.
function.name: GITHUB_ENCRYPT_ORG_DEV_ENV_SECRET

parameters.description: Request schema for creating or updating an organization's development environment secret with an encrypted value.

encrypted_value.description: Encrypted secret value, using the public key from 'Get an organization public key' (GET /orgs/{org}/codespaces/secrets/public-key). Use this if you have pre-encrypted the value yourself. If not provided and secret_value is given, encryption will be done automatically.
encrypted_value.title: Encrypted Value

key_id.description: ID of the public key (from GET /orgs/{org}/codespaces/secrets/public-key) used to encrypt the secret's value. If not provided, it will be fetched automatically when encrypting.
key_id.title: Key Id

secret_name.description: Name of the secret to create or update.
secret_name.title: Secret Name

secret_value.description: The plaintext value for your secret. This will be automatically encrypted using the organization's Codespaces public key before being sent to GitHub. If you already have an encrypted value, use the encrypted_value parameter instead.
secret_value.title: Secret Value

selected_repository_ids.description: Repository IDs that can access the secret; required if visibility is 'selected'.
selected_repository_ids.title: Selected Repository Ids

parameters.title: EncryptOrgDevEnvSecretRequest

function.name: GITHUB_EXPORT_SBOM_FOR_REPO

parameters.description: Request schema for `ExportSbomForRepo`

owner.description: The username or organization name that owns the repository. This field is not case-sensitive.
owner.title: Owner

repo.description: The name of the repository, without the `.git` extension. This field is not case-sensitive.
repo.title: Repo

parameters.title: ExportSbomForRepoRequest

function.description: Primary tool to find and search pull requests. Supports filtering by repository, author, state, labels, and merge status, and returns structured PR data for reliable use in workflows. GitHub search results are capped at ~1000 total items; narrow filters when totals approach this limit. `created_since`/`updated_since` filter on creation/update timestamps, not `merged_at`; apply merge-date filtering client-side.
function.name: GITHUB_FIND_PULL_REQUESTS

parameters.description: Request schema for finding pull requests with AI-friendly parameters.

assignee.description: Filter by PR assignee username. Use '@me' for current user. Must be a valid GitHub username (alphanumeric characters and hyphens only, cannot start or end with hyphen). No dots or other special characters allowed.
assignee.title: Assignee

author.description: Filter by PR author username. Must be a valid GitHub username (alphanumeric characters and hyphens only, cannot start or end with hyphen). No dots or other special characters allowed.
author.title: Author

created_since.description: Filter PRs created after this date (ISO 8601 format). Provide as UTC ISO 8601 timestamp to avoid off-by-one-day errors at range boundaries. Does not filter on `merged_at`.
created_since.title: Created Since

for_authenticated_user.description: Include private repositories accessible to the authenticated user in search results. When True without repo/owner, searches PRs involving you (author, assignee, mentions, commenter) across all accessible repos. When False without repo/owner, restricts to public repos only. When repo/owner is specified, this flag has no effect on scope. Set to True when searching private repositories; False searches all of GitHub and produces large noisy result sets.
for_authenticated_user.title: For Authenticated User

is_merged.description: Filter by merge status. True for merged PRs, False for unmerged, None for all.
is_merged.title: Is Merged

mentions.description: Filter by username mentioned in PR. Must be a valid GitHub username (alphanumeric characters and hyphens only, cannot start or end with hyphen). No dots or other special characters allowed.
mentions.title: Mentions

owner.description: Filter by repository owner (user or organization). Can be used alone to search all repositories for an owner, or combined with repo parameter to specify owner and repo separately. When both owner and repo are provided, the search is scoped to 'owner/repo'. When omitted along with repo, search scope is determined by for_authenticated_user flag. The owner must exist and repositories must be accessible with your token.
owner.title: Owner

page.description: Page number of results to fetch.
page.title: Page

per_page.description: Number of results per page (maximum 100). GitHub caps total search results at ~1000 items; iterate `page` until results fewer than `per_page` are returned, and add `repo`, `label`, or date filters when totals approach 1000.
per_page.title: Per Page

query.description: Optional search query for PR title, description, or commit messages. Supports GitHub search syntax including label filters (e.g., 'label:bug'), logical operators (AND, OR, NOT), and text search. If not provided, PRs will be listed based on other filter parameters. Type qualifiers (type:pr, is:pr, is:issue, type:issue) are automatically normalized. When using logical operators with qualifiers like labels, the query is automatically grouped for proper parsing. When omitting query, ensure at least one structured filter parameter is provided; passing no query and no filters may cause validation errors. Prefer dedicated parameters (`label`, `state`, `author`) over embedding qualifiers in the query string. Exact title matching is not supported; post-filter on title client-side if needed.
query.title: Query

raw_response.description: Return full API response if true, optimized response for AI agents if false.
raw_response.title: Raw Response

repo.description: Filter by specific repository. Can be provided in 'owner/repo' format (e.g., 'facebook/react'), or as just the repo name when used with the owner parameter. When owner and repo are both provided and have the same value, they are combined as 'owner/repo'. When omitted along with owner, search scope is determined by for_authenticated_user flag. The repository must exist and be accessible with your token. Prefer 'owner/repo' format; bare repo name without the `owner` parameter causes invalid repository format errors.
repo.title: Repo

sort.description: Field to sort results by. Set explicitly to 'updated' with `order=desc` when recency matters; default behavior may return best-match relevance ordering instead.
sort.title: Sort

updated_since.description: Filter PRs updated after this date (ISO 8601 format).
updated_since.title: Updated Since

parameters.title: FindPullRequestsRequest

function.description: AI-optimized repository search with smart filtering by language, stars, topics, and ownership. Builds intelligent search queries and returns clean, actionable repository data. Check `incomplete_results` in the response — when true, results are non-exhaustive. Search endpoints are rate-limited to ~30 requests/minute (vs. 5000/hour for general REST); apply backoff on 403/429. Total results are capped at ~1000 per query regardless of pagination.
function.name: GITHUB_FIND_REPOSITORIES

parameters.description: Request schema for finding repositories.

archived.description: Filter by archived status. None means include both.
archived.title: Archived

for_authenticated_user.description: Search only in repositories accessible to the authenticated user (including private repos). Requires token scopes `repo`/`read:org`; sparse results compared to GitHub UI typically indicate insufficient token scopes.
for_authenticated_user.title: For Authenticated User

fork_filter.description: How to handle forks: 'include' (include forks), 'exclude' (exclude forks), 'only' (only forks).
fork_filter.title: Fork Filter

max_stars.description: Maximum number of stars the repository should have.
max_stars.title: Max Stars

min_forks.description: Minimum number of forks the repository should have.
min_forks.title: Min Forks

min_stars.description: Minimum number of stars the repository should have.
min_stars.title: Min Stars

owner.description: Filter by specific user or organization. Do NOT use this parameter if your 'query' already contains 'user:', 'org:', or 'repo:' qualifiers - they will conflict. Use either 'owner' parameter OR search qualifiers in 'query', not both.
owner.title: Owner

page.description: Page number of results to fetch.
page.title: Page

per_page.description: Number of results per page (maximum 100). GitHub caps total results at ~1000 per query; for exhaustive searches, split by `language`, `topic`, `min_stars`/`max_stars`, or `owner`.
per_page.title: Per Page

query.description: Search query for repository name, description, or README content. Accepts both 'query' and 'q' as parameter names. Supports OR operator for alternatives (e.g., 'bug OR error'). Multi-word phrases in OR expressions are automatically quoted. GitHub limits OR expressions to 6 terms maximum; queries exceeding this limit are truncated. If using GitHub qualifiers like 'repo:', 'user:', or 'org:', they must be properly formatted (e.g., 'repo:owner/name', 'user:username', 'org:orgname'). Note: 'owner:' is not a valid GitHub qualifier; if used, it will be automatically transformed to 'user:'. Must be non-empty; blank strings trigger a 'Search query cannot be empty' validation error.
query.title: Query

raw_response.description: Return full API response if true, optimized response for AI agents if false.
raw_response.title: Raw Response

parameters.title: FindRepositoriesRequest

function.description: Tool to follow a GitHub organization using GraphQL. Use when you need to make the authenticated user follow an organization.
function.name: GITHUB_FOLLOW_ORGANIZATION_GRAPHQL

parameters.description:
Request parameters for following an organization via GitHub GraphQL.

This mutation allows the authenticated user to follow an organization.

clientMutationId.description: A unique identifier for the client performing the mutation. Used to track requests.
clientMutationId.title: Client Mutation Id

organizationId.description: The global GraphQL node ID of the organization to follow. Must be a valid organization node ID.
organizationId.title: Organization Id

parameters.title: FollowOrganizationGraphqlRequest

function.description: Allows the authenticated user to follow the GitHub user specified by `username`; this action is idempotent and the user cannot follow themselves.
function.name: GITHUB_FOLLOW_USER

parameters.description: Specifies the GitHub user to follow.

username.description: The GitHub username (handle) of the user to be followed by the authenticated user.
username.title: Username

parameters.title: FollowUserRequest
